calls.prompt_preview: Topic: Visiting Ljubljana CEFR Level: A2 Language: Slovene (sl)  ## Committed Pl
calls.response:
Here’s a concise 3‑day plan that follows the Pimsleur‑style sections and Krashen’s i+1 principle. Each day builds on the previous one, keeping the language just a step beyond the learner’s current A2 comfort zone while focusing on practical, travel‑related vocabulary for Ljubljana.

```json
{
  "days": [
    {
      "day": 1,
      "title": "Arrival & First Impressions",
      "focus": "Greetings, airport/railway vocabulary, basic self‑introduction",
      "collocations": [
        "dobro jutro",
        "kako ste?",
        "prispeti na letališče",
        "izhod iz vlaka",
        "potrebujem taksi",
        "kako se imenuješ",
        "od kje si"
      ],
      "learning_objective": "Learner can greet locals, ask and answer simple questions about identity and origin, and request basic transport assistance upon arrival.",
      "story_guidance": "Imagine stepping off the plane at Ljubljana Jože Pučnik Airport. You meet a friendly driver who offers to take you to the city centre. Use the collocations to greet him, tell him your name, and ask for directions to your hotel."
    },
    {
      "day": 2,
      "title": "Checking‑in & Exploring the Old Town",
      "focus": "Hotel reception dialogue, asking for information, describing location",
      "collocations": [
        "rezervacija sobe",
        "prosim, ključ",
        "kako daleč je do starega mesta",
        "katera je najboljša restavracija",
        "imam rezervacijo",
        "ali je zajtrk vključen",
        "kako se peljem do Tromostovja"
      ],
      "learning_objective": "Learner can complete a hotel check‑in, request amenities, and ask for simple directions to nearby attractions.",
      "story_guidance": "After the driver drops you at your hotel, you go to the reception desk. Use the collocations to confirm your reservation, ask about breakfast, and get directions to the iconic Triple Bridge (Tromostovje) and a recommended restaurant."
    },
    {
      "day": 3,
      "title": "Dining & Saying Goodbye",
      "focus": "Restaurant ordering, expressing preferences, polite farewells",
      "collocations": [
        "prosim, meniji",
        "rad bi poskusil",
        "lahko priporočite",
        "račun, prosim",
        "hvala za pomoč",
        "adijo, se vidimo",
        "kako pridem na letališče"
      ],
      "learning_objective": "Learner can order a meal, ask for recommendations, request the bill, and say goodbye while arranging transport back to the airport.",
      "story_guidance": "You sit down at a cozy café in the Old Town. Use the collocations to ask for the menu, request a local specialty, request the bill, thank the staff, and finally ask the waiter how to get back to the airport for your departure."
    }
  ]
}
```

calls.prompt_preview: Topic: Visiting Ljubljana CEFR Level: A2 Language: Slovene (sl)  ## Committed Pl
calls.response:
Here are three additional days that continue the visit to Ljubljana, introducing slightly more advanced vocabulary for restaurants, shopping, and cultural activities while still respecting the A2 → B1 transition.

```json
{
  "days": [
    {
      "day": 4,
      "title": "Taste of Slovene Cuisine",
      "focus": "Restaurant dialogue – ordering dishes, asking about ingredients, expressing preferences and dietary needs",
      "collocations": [
        "žlička olja",
        "brez glutena",
        "priporočate specialiteto",
        "katero jed je najboljša",
        "rad imam pikantno",
        "lahko prinesete račun",
        "ali je vsebina vegetarijanska"
      ],
      "learning_objective": "Learner can read a menu, request specific ingredients or modifications, ask the waiter for recommendations, and politely request the bill, demonstrating ability to handle dietary restrictions.",
      "story_guidance": "You are seated at a traditional restaurant near Prešeren Square. Use the collocations to ask the waiter which dish is the house specialty, request a gluten‑free version of a soup, and finally ask for the check while thanking the staff."
    },
    {
      "day": 5,
      "title": "Shopping in the City Centre",
      "focus": "Retail interactions – asking for sizes, prices, trying on clothes, negotiating discounts, and handling returns",
      "collocations": [
        "kakšna je cena",
        "ali je na voljo v drugi velikosti",
        "kako se plača",
        "imam popustno kodo",
        "rad bi poskusil to majico",
        "kako vrnem izdelek",
        "ali sprejemate kartice"
      ],
      "learning_objective": "Learner can inquire about product details, request different sizes, discuss payment methods, and manage a simple return, enabling confident shopping experiences.",
      "story_guidance": "Imagine browsing a boutique on Trubarjeva street. Use the collocations to ask the shop assistant about the price of a sweater, try it on in a different size, ask if a discount code works, and learn how to return an item if needed."
    },
    {
      "day": 6,
      "title": "Culture & Farewell",
      "focus": "Visiting museums and cultural sites – buying tickets, asking about opening hours, describing impressions, and saying goodbye to new friends",
      "collocations": [
        "kako dolgo traja ogled",
        "katera je cena vstopnice",
        "kdaj se odpre muzej",
        "zelo mi je všeč razstava",
        "lahko priporočite drugo lokacijo",
        "se vidimo spet",
        "hvala za prijaznost"
      ],
      "learning_objective": "Learner can purchase tickets, inquire about schedules, express opinions about exhibits, and bid farewell to acquaintances, consolidating both functional and expressive language skills.",
      "story_guidance": "Spend your last afternoon at the National Museum of Slovenia. Use the collocations to buy a ticket, ask how long the tour lasts, comment on a favorite exhibit, get a suggestion for another place to visit, and say goodbye to a fellow traveler you met at the museum."
    }
  ]
}
```
